entries.problem: A feature branch deployment for The Ascent was returning 500 errors due to Redis connection timeouts (`django_redis.exceptions.ConnectionInterrupted: Redis TimeoutError: Timeout writing to socket`). The root cause was that smoke tests were hitting the previous release of the feature branch rather than the newly deployed one, because the cache instance restart during deployment caused the old running release to flood with Redis disconnect errors.
entries.solution: The feature namespace was nuked and restarted fresh, which resolved the issue. The underlying problem was a stale feature namespace where the previous release was interfering with the new deployment's smoke tests during the Redis cache restart cycle.

Hey there CLOPS :cyclops:

It looks like we're getting a 500 on a feature branch this morning that we can't really resolve on our own. Link to the jenkins console log here:
<https://leroy.fool.com/job/apps/job/the-ascent/job/the-ascent-deploy/15609/console>

Something I noticed with this though too is that it's running on python 3.9? Python 3.9 support is being deprecated across libraries, so that _might_ be why we're seeing the 500s, but not entirely clear.

We don't have any references to python 3.9 in our repo as far as I can tell, but something in jenkins looks like it's still building python 3.9
when I visit a page on that feature branch I get a stack trace and a 500 error
<https://the-ascent-feature-act-2923-rmv-podcast.development.fool.com/money/credit-cards/chase/chase-sapphire-preferred-card-review/>

That was in the before times when we had more cloudops support and they did things to the apps like that. Which again is why I'm coming to y'all for support on this redis issue, but if you are floundering I can flounder on my own as well.

We're here to help, but not many folks are still in cloudops from 2022 so we're discovering and asking questions the same as you :slightly_smiling_face:

One thing I'm noticing is that the smoke tests are _definitely_ pointing at the previous release of the feature branch, not the current, new one that Jenkins is actually deploying. I can see this in the container logs. What's happening is the cache instance is being restarted on deploy, which sends the previous release that's running into a frenzy of redis disconnect errors until it restarts, which causes the smoke tests which shouldn't even be going there to fail / flail
I'm going to nuke this feature namespace and start fresh
That seems to have fixed it.
